preview_text:
The model forecasts Ecuador as a heavy favorite with an 82% win probability, leaving Curaçao with just 1% odds of victory. This represents an asymmetric matchup where the underdog faces a steep climb regardless of formation or preparation.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects decisiveness rather than competitive balance. Ecuador's substantial lead in win probability means the model envisions a match tilted toward a conclusive result, though the 16% draw probability indicates that a stalemate remains a realistic outcome. A Curaçao win sits at the tail of the distribution, suggesting near-zero expectation of an upset.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably on the draw outcome. The market assigns 10%, while the model's bias-corrected forecast sits at 16%—a meaningful gap suggesting the market prices draws as less likely in this pairing. Both sources align on Ecuador's dominance but differ on the split between draws and home-team wins. The dashboard makes no claim as to which assessment of draw likelihood is correct.
